channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I Built and Published a .NET NuGet Package for the Giant SMS API
item.description: A while back, I needed to integrate SMS into a .NET project. Giant SMS had a REST API, but no official .NET client. The only existing library was a PHP one from 6–7 years ago, and it only covered...

item.title: I Built a Free Open-Source EU AI Act / NIST AI RMF / ISO 42001 Crosswalk Tool - Here Is What I Found
item.description: Every week I see the same question in AI governance communities: "We already have NIST AI RMF implemented. Does that cover our EU AI Act obligations?" The honest answer is: sometimes yes,...

item.title: Why I built a native libmpv IPTV player for Windows — an HDR tone-mapping deep-dive
item.description: Up front, so there's no confusion: the app I'm describing (Nightmare TV) is a player only. You bring your own M3U / Xtream Codes playlist — it ships with no channels and no content. This post is...

item.title: Howdy. I built budget controls for AI agents, does this solve a problem you actually have?
item.description: been building AI agent infrastructure for the past few months. The two things that kept biting me — and kept coming up when I talked to other devs building agents — were runaway costs and agents...

item.title: Building an AI Short Video Generator: Why the Workflow Needs Skills, Not Just Prompts
item.description: Most AI short-form video demos skip the boring part. They show a finished TikTok, Reel, or YouTube Short. Maybe they show the prompt. Maybe they show the generated script or the final render. But...

item.title: Learn SQL Once, Use It for 30 Years: Why the Skill Doesn't Expire
item.description: A post titled "Learn SQL Once, Use It for 30 Years" hit the front page of r/programming this week (307 points, 48 comments). The claim sounds like the kind of thing a database vendor would put on...

item.title: Congress still can’t decide what to do about warrantless surveillance
item.description: The deadline to reauthorize Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is coming up a week from now on June 12th, and legislators seem no closer to reaching a deal. If this sounds...

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications officially close in 3 days
item.description: Applications for Startup Battlefield 200 officially close on June 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't wait any longer. Secure your shot at competing on the Disrupt Stage at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 this...

item.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
item.description: In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.

item.title: IronWorm and New Miasma Worm Variant Hit npm in Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Multiple software supply chain attacks have hit the npm ecosystem, with threat actors using both malicious and poisoned versions of over 50 legitimate packages to distribute a Rust-based...

item.title: The Sonos Era 100 speaker is down to its lowest price in months

item.title: This is your laptop… on AI
item.description: We're now deep into developer conference season, and one of the themes so far is the relentless conviction from Big Tech companies that AI is going to change everything about how we do everything....

item.title: What happens when your phone is confiscated at the airport
item.description: Even if you've done nothing wrong, it's never a good idea to hand your phone to the cops. But international travelers at American airports often have no choice - even if they're US citizens. When...

item.title: New York lawmakers pass one-year ban on new data centers
item.description: The New York State legislature passed a one-year moratorium on new large data centers, the first statewide ban of its kind if Democratic Governor Kathy Hochul signs it into law. Lawmakers behind...

item.title: Has Microsoft Lost Its Mojo (Again)?
item.description: Microsoft’s AI products aren’t selling, and Github’s been plagued with troubles. WIRED spoke with VP Scott Hanselman about whether the company is in catch-up mode.

item.title: Android Spyware Asin Targets Arabic Users via Fake News, PDF and War Map Apps
item.description: Arabic-speaking users have emerged as the target of a new Android spyware codenamed Asin, according to findings from ESET. The Slovakian cybersecurity company said it first detected the malware...

item.title: The token bill comes due: Inside the industry scramble to manage AI’s runaway costs
item.description: "The whole conversation shifted from tokenmaxxing and 'go fast' to 'we need guardrails, how do we control this?'"

item.title: This AI startup says it can tell if a script will make a hit film

item.title: AI Worm
item.description: Researchers have prototyped an AI-powered internet worm. The coolest thing about the prototype is that it carries its own LLM with it, and runs it on computers that have been broken into. This is...
